title: dynTools: Dynamic Modeling Utilities
text: Utility functions data preparation fitting dynamic models.

title: dynTools: Dynamic Modeling Utilities
text: can install released version dynTools GitHub :
code: if (!require("pak")) install.packages("pak") pak::pkg_install("jeksterslab/dynTools")

title: dynTools: Dynamic Modeling Utilities
text: See GitHub Pages package documentation.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Pesigan, . J. . (2026). dynTools: Dynamic Modeling Utilities. R package version 0.0.0.9000. https://github.com/ijapesigan/dynTools
code: @Manual{,   author = {Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan},   title = {dynTools: Dynamic Modeling Utilities},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000}, }

title: Dynamic Modeling Utilities
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan 2026-06-12

title: Dynamic Modeling Utilities
text: Utility functions data preparation fitting dynamic models.

title: Dynamic Modeling Utilities
text: can install released version dynTools GitHub :
code: if (!require("pak")) install.packages("pak") pak::pkg_install("jeksterslab/dynTools")

title: Dynamic Modeling Utilities
text: See GitHub Pages package documentation.

title: Check Dynamic Modeling Data — CheckDynData
text: function checks whether data frame variables structure required dynamic modeling utility functions.

title: Check Dynamic Modeling Data — CheckDynData
code: CheckDynData(   data,   id,   time,   observed,   covariates = NULL,   require_unique = TRUE,   require_numeric_time = TRUE,   require_numeric_observed = TRUE,   require_numeric_covariates = FALSE,   min_rows = 2L )

title: Check Dynamic Modeling Data — CheckDynData
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables data. covariates Character vector. vector character strings names covariates data. require_unique Logical. TRUE, require unique id-time combinations. require_numeric_time Logical. TRUE, require time variable numeric. require_numeric_observed Logical. TRUE, require observed variables numeric. require_numeric_covariates Logical. TRUE, require covariates numeric. min_rows Positive integer. Minimum number rows required per ID.

title: Check Dynamic Modeling Data — CheckDynData
text: Returns TRUE invisibly checks pass.

title: Check Dynamic Modeling Data — CheckDynData
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Check Dynamic Modeling Data — CheckDynData
code: data <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:2, each = 3),   time = rep(1:3, times = 2),   y1 = rnorm(6),   y2 = rnorm(6) ) CheckDynData(   data = data,   id = "id",   time = "time",   observed = c("y1", "y2") )

what: Delete for NAs in Initial Row By ID — DeleteInitialNA
title: Delete for NAs in Initial Row By ID — DeleteInitialNA
text: function removes initial rows ID contain missing values. process repeated first row per ID longer missing observations.

title: Delete for NAs in Initial Row By ID — DeleteInitialNA
code: DeleteInitialNA(data, id, time, observed, covariates = NULL)

title: Delete for NAs in Initial Row By ID — DeleteInitialNA
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables data. covariates Character vector. vector character strings names covariates data.

title: Delete for NAs in Initial Row By ID — DeleteInitialNA
text: Returns data frame.

title: Delete for NAs in Initial Row By ID — DeleteInitialNA
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Delete for NAs in Initial Row By ID — DeleteInitialNA

what: Summarize Time Intervals by ID — DeltaTByID
title: Summarize Time Intervals by ID — DeltaTByID
text: function returns diagnostic table describing spacing time variable within ID.

title: Summarize Time Intervals by ID — DeltaTByID
code: DeltaTByID(   data,   id,   time,   observed = NULL,   covariates = NULL,   tolerance = sqrt(.Machine$double.eps) )

title: Summarize Time Intervals by ID — DeltaTByID
text: data Data frame. data frame object. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. Optional vector character strings names observed variables data. covariates Character vector. Optional vector character strings names covariates data. tolerance Numeric. Tolerance used determine whether time intervals regular.

title: Summarize Time Intervals by ID — DeltaTByID
text: Returns data frame one row per ID.

title: Summarize Time Intervals by ID — DeltaTByID
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Summarize Time Intervals by ID — DeltaTByID
code: data <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:2, each = 3),   time = c(1, 2, 3, 1, 3, 4),   y = rnorm(6) ) data #>   id time           y #> 1  1    1  2.06502490 #> 2  1    2 -1.63098940 #> 3  1    3  0.51242695 #> 4  2    1 -1.86301149 #> 5  2    3 -0.52201251 #> 6  2    4 -0.05260191  DeltaTByID(   data = data,   id = "id",   time = "time",   observed = "y" ) #>   id n_rows n_intervals time_min time_max min_delta_t median_delta_t #> 1  1      3           2        1        3           1            1.0 #> 2  2      3           2        1        4           1            1.5 #>   max_delta_t n_unique_delta_t regular #> 1           1                1    TRUE #> 2           2                2   FALSE

what: Detrend Observed Variables by ID — DetrendByID
title: Detrend Observed Variables by ID — DetrendByID
text: function removes polynomial time trends observed variables within ID. Missing observed values ignored estimating trend remain missing residualized variables.

title: Detrend Observed Variables by ID — DetrendByID
code: DetrendByID(   data,   id,   time,   observed,   covariates = NULL,   degree = 1L,   replace = FALSE,   prefix = "detrend" )

title: Detrend Observed Variables by ID — DetrendByID
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables data. covariates Character vector. vector character strings names covariates data. degree Non-negative integer. Degree polynomial trend. Use degree = 0 mean centering ID. replace Logical. TRUE, replace observed variables residualized variables. FALSE, append residualized variables data frame. prefix Character string. Prefix residualized variables replace = FALSE.

title: Detrend Observed Variables by ID — DetrendByID
text: Returns data frame.

title: Detrend Observed Variables by ID — DetrendByID
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Detrend Observed Variables by ID — DetrendByID
code: data <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:2, each = 4),   time = rep(1:4, times = 2),   y = rep(1:4, times = 2) ) data #>   id time y #> 1  1    1 1 #> 2  1    2 2 #> 3  1    3 3 #> 4  1    4 4 #> 5  2    1 1 #> 6  2    2 2 #> 7  2    3 3 #> 8  2    4 4  DetrendByID(   data = data,   id = "id",   time = "time",   observed = "y",   degree = 1 ) #>   id time y detrend_y #> 1  1    1 1         0 #> 2  1    2 2         0 #> 3  1    3 3         0 #> 4  1    4 4         0 #> 5  2    1 1         0 #> 6  2    2 2         0 #> 7  2    3 3         0 #> 8  2    4 4         0

what: Create Elapsed Time by ID — ElapsedTimeByID
title: Create Elapsed Time by ID — ElapsedTimeByID
text: function creates elapsed-time variable within ID. date-time variables, elapsed time computed difftime(). numeric time variables, elapsed time computed subtraction, optionally converting input_units units.

title: Create Elapsed Time by ID — ElapsedTimeByID
code: ElapsedTimeByID(   data,   id,   time,   output = "time_elapsed",   units = "days",   origin = c("by_id", "global"),   input_units = NULL,   replace = FALSE )

title: Create Elapsed Time by ID — ElapsedTimeByID
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. output Character string. Name elapsed-time variable. units Character string. Units output elapsed time. origin Character string. origin = "by_id", ID's origin minimum non-missing time. origin = "global", IDs use global minimum non-missing time. input_units Character string NULL. Units numeric input time. NULL, numeric time assumed already desired output scale subtraction performed. replace Logical. TRUE, replace original time variable. FALSE, append output.

title: Create Elapsed Time by ID — ElapsedTimeByID
text: Returns data frame.

title: Create Elapsed Time by ID — ElapsedTimeByID
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Create Elapsed Time by ID — ElapsedTimeByID

what: Filter Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — FilterByID
title: Filter Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — FilterByID
text: function removes IDs satisfy minimum data requirements.

title: Filter Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — FilterByID
code: FilterByID(   data,   id,   time,   observed,   covariates = NULL,   min_rows = 2L,   min_complete = 2L,   max_prop_missing = 1,   allow_initial_na = TRUE,   allow_all_missing = FALSE )

title: Filter Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — FilterByID
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables data. covariates Character vector. vector character strings names covariates data. min_rows Non-negative integer. Minimum number rows required per ID. min_complete Non-negative integer. Minimum number complete observed rows required per ID. max_prop_missing Numeric. Maximum allowed proportion missing values across observed variables. allow_initial_na Logical. FALSE, remove IDs initial row contains missing values. allow_all_missing Logical. FALSE, remove IDs observed values missing.

title: Filter Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — FilterByID
text: Returns data frame.

title: Filter Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — FilterByID
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Filter Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — FilterByID
code: data <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:3, each = 3),   time = rep(1:3, times = 3),   y = c(1, 2, 3, NA, NA, 4, NA, NA, NA) ) data #>   id time  y #> 1  1    1  1 #> 2  1    2  2 #> 3  1    3  3 #> 4  2    1 NA #> 5  2    2 NA #> 6  2    3  4 #> 7  3    1 NA #> 8  3    2 NA #> 9  3    3 NA  FilterByID(   data = data,   id = "id",   time = "time",   observed = "y",   min_complete = 2 ) #>   id time y #> 1  1    1 1 #> 2  1    2 2 #> 3  1    3 3

what: Create Lagged Variables by ID — LagByID
title: Create Lagged Variables by ID — LagByID
text: function creates lagged observed variables within ID. Lagged values never cross ID boundaries.

title: Create Lagged Variables by ID — LagByID
code: LagByID(data, id, time, observed, covariates = NULL, lags = 1L, prefix = "lag")

title: Create Lagged Variables by ID — LagByID
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables data. covariates Character vector. vector character strings names covariates data. lags Positive integer vector. Lags create. prefix Character string. Prefix lagged variable names.

title: Create Lagged Variables by ID — LagByID
text: Returns data frame.

title: Create Lagged Variables by ID — LagByID

title: Create Lagged Variables by ID — LagByID
Make Clock Time — MakeClockTime
text: function combines date clock time POSIXct vector. Clock time can supplied decimal hours, example 13.5, character time, example "13:30" "13:30:00".

title: Make Clock Time — MakeClockTime
code: MakeClockTime(   date,   time,   tz = "UTC",   date_formats = c("%m/%d/%y", "%m/%d/%Y", "%Y-%m-%d"),   invalid = c("NA", "error") )

title: Make Clock Time — MakeClockTime
text: date Date vector. Dates can supplied Date, POSIXt, character values. time Numeric character vector. Clock time decimal hours, "HH:MM", "HH:MM:SS" format. tz Character string. Time zone passed .POSIXct(). date_formats Character vector. Date formats used parse character dates. invalid Character string. invalid = "NA", invalid clock times returned NA. invalid = "error", invalid clock times throw error.

title: Make Clock Time — MakeClockTime
text: Returns POSIXct vector.

title: Make Clock Time — MakeClockTime
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Make Clock Time — MakeClockTime
code: MakeClockTime(   date = "2020-01-02",   time = 13.5,   tz = "America/New_York" ) #> [1] "2020-01-02 13:30:00 EST"  MakeClockTime(   date = "01/02/20",   time = "13:30",   tz = "America/New_York" ) #> [1] "2020-01-02 13:30:00 EST"

what: Plot Observed Variables by ID — PlotByID
title: Plot Observed Variables by ID — PlotByID
text: function creates one time-series plot observed variable. Within plot, trajectories overlaid ID.

title: Plot Observed Variables by ID — PlotByID
code: PlotByID(   data,   id,   time,   observed,   ids = NULL,   times = NULL,   type = "b",   pch = NULL,   lty = NULL,   col = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   main = NULL,   xlim = NULL,   ylim = NULL,   legend = FALSE,   ask = NULL,   ... )

title: Plot Observed Variables by ID — PlotByID
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables data. ids Optional vector. ID values plot. NULL, available IDs plotted. times Optional vector time values. NULL, available time points plotted. supplied, range times used subset data. example, times = c(0, 9) plots rows time values 0 9, inclusive. type Character string. Plot type passed graphics::lines(). Default "b". pch Optional vector plotting characters. NULL, plotting characters generated automatically ID. Recycled match number plotted IDs. lty Optional vector line types. NULL, line types generated automatically ID. Recycled match number plotted IDs. col Optional vector colors. NULL, colors generated automatically ID. Recycled match number plotted IDs. xlab Optional character string. Label x-axis. NULL, name time variable used. ylab Optional character string. Label y-axis. NULL, observed variable name used. main Optional character string. Plot title prefix. NULL, observed variable name used. xlim Optional vector length 2. Limits x-axis. ylim Optional numeric vector length 2. Limits y-axis. NULL, limits computed separately observed variable. legend Logical. TRUE, add legend identifying IDs. ask Logical. TRUE, ask drawing next observed-variable plot. default TRUE interactive sessions plotting one observed variable. ... Additional arguments passed graphics::lines().

title: Plot Observed Variables by ID — PlotByID
text: Invisibly returns IDs plotted.

title: Plot Observed Variables by ID — PlotByID
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Plot Observed Variables by ID — PlotByID
Regularize Time by ID — RegularizeTimeByID
text: function inserts rows missing values time follows regular grid. global grid uses time sequence every ID. ID-specific grid uses ID's minimum maximum observed time values.

title: Regularize Time by ID — RegularizeTimeByID
code: RegularizeTimeByID(   data,   id,   time,   observed,   covariates = NULL,   delta_t,   grid = c("global", "by_id") )

title: Regularize Time by ID — RegularizeTimeByID
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables data. covariates Character vector. vector character strings names covariates data. delta_t Positive number. Time interval. grid Character string. grid = "global", use one time grid global minimum global maximum time value. grid = "by_id", use ID-specific time grid ID's minimum maximum time value.

title: Regularize Time by ID — RegularizeTimeByID
text: Returns data frame.

title: Regularize Time by ID — RegularizeTimeByID
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Regularize Time by ID — RegularizeTimeByID
code: data <- data.frame(   id = c(1, 1, 2, 2),   time = c(1, 3, 1, 2),   y = c(10, 12, 20, 21) ) data #>   id time  y #> 1  1    1 10 #> 2  1    3 12 #> 3  2    1 20 #> 4  2    2 21  RegularizeTimeByID(   data = data,   id = "id",   time = "time",   observed = "y",   delta_t = 1,   grid = "by_id" ) #>   id time  y #> 1  1    1 10 #> 2  1    2 NA #> 3  1    3 12 #> 4  2    1 20 #> 5  2    2 21

title: Replace Missing-Value Codes — ReplaceMissingCode
text: function replaces user-specified missing-value codes NA data frame. replacement applied column column preserves original column classes whenever possible.

title: Replace Missing-Value Codes — ReplaceMissingCode
code: ReplaceMissingCode(data, values = c(-999, "-999"), columns = names(data))

title: Replace Missing-Value Codes — ReplaceMissingCode
text: data Data frame. values Vector. Values replace NA. columns Character vector. Names columns replacement applied. NULL, columns modified.

title: Replace Missing-Value Codes — ReplaceMissingCode
text: Returns data frame.

title: Replace Missing-Value Codes — ReplaceMissingCode
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Replace Missing-Value Codes — ReplaceMissingCode
code: data <- data.frame(   id = 1:3,   y = c(1, -999, 3),   x = c("a", "-999", "c"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) data #>   id    y    x #> 1  1    1    a #> 2  2 -999 -999 #> 3  3    3    c  ReplaceMissingCode(   data = data,   values = c(-999, "-999") ) #>   id  y    x #> 1  1  1    a #> 2  2 NA <NA> #> 3  3  3    c

title: Resolve Duplicate ID-Time Rows — ResolveDuplicateIDTime
text: function resolves exact duplicate id-time rows using simple deterministic rule. useful inserting missing rows time grid grid-completion functions generally require unique id-time combinations.

title: Resolve Duplicate ID-Time Rows — ResolveDuplicateIDTime
code: ResolveDuplicateIDTime(   data,   id,   time,   observed = NULL,   covariates = NULL,   method = c("first", "last", "max_complete"),   order_by = NULL,   decreasing = FALSE )

title: Resolve Duplicate ID-Time Rows — ResolveDuplicateIDTime
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables data. covariates Character vector. vector character strings names covariates data. method Character string. Rule used choose one row duplicated id-time group. method = "first", keep first row sorting id time. method = "last", keep last row sorting id time. method = "max_complete", keep row largest number non-missing values observed. order_by Character vector. Optional columns used break ties main method rule. decreasing Logical. TRUE, sort order_by columns decreasing order.

title: Resolve Duplicate ID-Time Rows — ResolveDuplicateIDTime
text: Returns data frame unique id-time rows.

title: Resolve Duplicate ID-Time Rows — ResolveDuplicateIDTime
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Resolve Duplicate ID-Time Rows — ResolveDuplicateIDTime
code: data <- data.frame(   id = c(1, 1, 1),   time = c(0, 0, 1),   y1 = c(NA, 1, 2),   y2 = c(NA, 1, 3) ) data #>   id time y1 y2 #> 1  1    0 NA NA #> 2  1    0  1  1 #> 3  1    1  2  3  ResolveDuplicateIDTime(   data = data,   id = "id",   time = "time",   observed = c("y1", "y2"),   method = "max_complete" ) #>   id time y1 y2 #> 1  1    0  1  1 #> 2  1    1  2  3

title: Round Clock Time — RoundClockTime
text: function rounds, floors, ceilings clock times specified unit. supports POSIXt, Date, numeric time values.

title: Round Clock Time — RoundClockTime
code: RoundClockTime(   x,   unit = "hour",   method = c("round", "floor", "ceiling"),   origin = "1970-01-01",   tz = "UTC" )

title: Round Clock Time — RoundClockTime
text: x POSIXt, Date, numeric vector. unit Character string. Unit round . Supported values "second", "minute", "hour", "day", plural forms. method Character string. One "round", "floor", "ceiling". origin Origin numeric time values. used x numeric. tz Character string. Time zone used numeric time values converted POSIXct.

title: Round Clock Time — RoundClockTime
text: Returns vector rounded times.

title: Round Clock Time — RoundClockTime
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Round Clock Time — RoundClockTime
code: x <- as.POSIXct(   "2020-01-01 10:31:00",   tz = "America/New_York" )  RoundClockTime(   x = x,   unit = "hour" ) #> [1] "2020-01-01 11:00:00 EST"

title: Scale by ID — ScaleByID

title: Scale by ID — ScaleByID
code: ScaleByID(   data,   id,   time,   observed,   covariates = NULL,   scale = TRUE,   obs_skip = NULL,   cov_skip = NULL )

title: Scale by ID — ScaleByID
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables data. covariates Character vector. vector character strings names covariates data. scale Logical. scale = TRUE, standardize id. scale = FALSE, mean center id. obs_skip Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables skip centering/scaling. cov_skip Character vector. vector character strings names covariates skip centering/scaling.

title: Summarize Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — SummaryByID
text: data Data frame. data frame object data potentially multiple subjects contain column subject ID numbers (.e., ID variable), column indicating subject-specific measurement occasions (.e., TIME variable), least one column observed values. id Character string. character string name ID variable data. time Character string. character string name TIME variable data. observed Character vector. vector character strings names observed variables data. covariates Character vector. vector character strings names covariates data.

title: Summarize Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — SummaryByID
text: Returns data frame one row per ID.

title: Summarize Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — SummaryByID
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Summarize Dynamic Modeling Data by ID — SummaryByID
code: data <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:2, each = 3),   time = rep(1:3, times = 2),   y1 = c(1, NA, 3, 4, 5, 6),   y2 = c(1, 2, 3, NA, NA, NA) ) data #>   id time y1 y2 #> 1  1    1  1  1 #> 2  1    2 NA  2 #> 3  1    3  3  3 #> 4  2    1  4 NA #> 5  2    2  5 NA #> 6  2    3  6 NA  SummaryByID(   data = data,   id = "id",   time = "time",   observed = c("y1", "y2") ) #>   id n_rows n_complete n_missing prop_missing time_min time_max n_unique_time #> 1  1      3          2         1    0.1666667        1        3             3 #> 2  2      3          0         3    0.5000000        1        3             3 #>   n_duplicate_time initial_na all_missing #> 1                0      FALSE       FALSE #> 2                0       TRUE       FALSE
